Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, hormonal therapy, and chemotherapy
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'INTRODUCTION' on page 1 further clarifies that 'darolutamide' is an 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor'. 'Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)' is a hormonal therapy, and 'docetaxel' is a chemotherapy agent. Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 consists of an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (darolutamide), hormonal therapy (ADT), and chemotherapy (docetaxel).
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Control Arm.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1 further clarifies: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel. The darolutamide group... compared with the placebo group.' The CONSORT flow diagram on page 3 also explicitly labels one arm as 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT'.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This indicates two arms: one with darolutamide and one with placebo, both in combination with ADT and docetaxel. The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further confirms this by stating patients were 'randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The title also indicates 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel'. Figure 1 on page 3 explicitly labels the experimental arm as 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The document reports 'median survival' and 'median treatment duration' in months, but does not explicitly state the 'Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)' for the entire study population or any subgroups.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 3, Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT', it states 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)'.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'RESULTS OF 1,305 patients, 1,005 (77%) had high-volume disease and 912 (70%) had high-risk disease.' On page 3, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'Patient Characteristics', it states: 'As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned, and 1,305 patients were included in the full analysis set of ARASENS (Fig 1).' Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', has a column 'All Patients (N = 1,305)'. This consistently indicates 1,305 participants were included in the analysis.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel; Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: Found in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract and 'INTRODUCTION' section, stating patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'.

NCT.evidence: Found under 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' on page 12.

PubMed ID.evidence: A specific PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document. The document provides a DOI and journal information, but not a PubMed ID.

Trial Name.evidence: Found in the title of the article: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial' on page 1.

Author.evidence: Found the list of authors starting with 'Maha Hussain, MD' on page 1. Using 'et al.' for brevity as requested for a list of names.

Year.evidence: Found '© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology' on page 1.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is structured as a full research paper with an abstract, introduction, methods, results, discussion, and references, and is labeled 'original reports' on page 1.

Phase.evidence: Stated in the title 'Phase III ARASENS Trial' on page 1 and confirmed in the 'METHODS' section on page 2: 'ARASENS was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase III trial'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The 'Key Objective' and 'Knowledge Generated' sections on page 2 explicitly state that this paper presents 'subgroup analyses of ARASENS', indicating it is a follow-up analysis of an existing trial.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 3, under 'Study Assessments', the document states that secondary efficacy end points included 'time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' and 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire'. These are measures related to quality of life.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: On page 3, in the 'Study Assessments' section, 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire' is listed as a secondary efficacy end point. This is a standardized questionnaire used to assess aspects of quality of life in cancer patients.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Found in TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS, on page 5. Under the 'All Patients' column, for 'Darolutamide (n = 651)', the 'Age, median, years (range)' is 67.0 (41-89).

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS, on page 5. Under the 'All Patients' column, for 'Placebo (n = 654)', the 'Age, median, years (range)' is 67.0 (42-85).

Control Arm - N.evidence: The CONSORT flow diagram (FIG 1) on page 3 indicates that 1,306 patients were randomly assigned. The 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm, which serves as the control arm, had 'n = 655' patients randomly assigned to it.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: On page 11, in the 'DISCUSSION' section, it states: 'median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document provides median treatment durations for the placebo arm in various subgroups (e.g., 15.3 months for high-volume, 26.5 months for low-volume on page 4), but an overall median on-treatment duration for the control arm of the ARASENS trial is not explicitly reported as a single value.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states, "We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." On page 2, under 'Timing of metastatic presentation', it mentions "The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease)" and later, "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Appendix Fig A1 on page 16 explicitly shows OS data for 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis' with 'De novo' (synchronous) and 'Recurrent' (metachronous) subgroups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states, "We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." On page 2, under 'Timing of metastatic presentation', it mentions "developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease)" and later, "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Appendix Fig A1 on page 16 explicitly shows OS data for 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis' with 'De novo' (synchronous) and 'Recurrent' (metachronous) subgroups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states, "We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." It further defines, "High-volume disease was defined as visceral metastases and/or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond the vertebral column/pelvis." The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 reports OS for patients with high-volume disease (HR, 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82). Table 1 (page 5), Table 2 (page 8), Table 3 (page 9), and Table 4 (page 10) all include columns for 'High Volume' subgroups. Figure 2A (page 6) also presents OS for high-volume disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states, "We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 mentions, "and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13)." The 'CONCLUSION' on page 1 also refers to "high-risk/low-risk metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Table 1 (page 5), Table 2 (page 8), Table 3 (page 9), and Table 4 (page 10) all include columns for 'Low Volume' subgroups. Figure 2B (page 6) also presents OS for low-volume disease.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics', under 'ECOG PS, No. (%)', row '1', column 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' on page 5. PS 2 was not reported separately in the table.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics', under 'ECOG PS, No. (%)', row '1', column 'Placebo (n = 508)' on page 5. PS 2 was not reported separately in the table.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, page 5, under 'High Volumea' column, 'Darolutamide' sub-column header (n=497). The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm for primary end point analysis was 651 (from Fig 1, page 3). Percentage calculated as (497/651)*100.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, page 5, under 'High Volumea' column, 'Placebo' sub-column header (n=508). The total number of patients in the Placebo arm for primary end point analysis was 654 (from Fig 1, page 3). Percentage calculated as (508/654)*100.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, page 5, under 'Low Volumea' column, 'Placebo' sub-column header (n=146). The total number of patients in the Placebo arm for primary end point analysis was 654 (from Fig 1, page 3). Percentage calculated as (146/654)*100.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 1, the 'METHODS' section states that patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel', indicating docetaxel was part of the initial treatment for both arms. Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT', shows 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)' out of 'Patients randomly assigned (n = 651)' for this arm. Therefore, 651 out of 651 (100%) participants in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 1, the 'METHODS' section states that patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel', indicating docetaxel was part of the initial treatment for both arms. Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT', shows 'Received allocated treatment (n = 651)' out of 'Patients randomly assigned (n = 655)' for this arm. Therefore, 651 out of 655 (99.4%) participants in the control arm received docetaxel.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, it is explicitly stated: 'OS was the primary end point.' The abbreviation 'OS' is defined as 'overall survival' in the abbreviations list on the same page.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' on page 5. Under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', for 'De novo mHSPC', the total for the Darolutamide arm is calculated by summing the 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' and 'Darolutamide (n = 154)' columns: 432 + 126 = 558. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is 558/651 = 85.7%.

Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' on page 5. Under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', for 'De novo mHSPC', the total for the Placebo arm is calculated by summing the 'Placebo (n = 508)' and 'Placebo (n = 146)' columns: 445 + 121 = 566. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is 566/654 = 86.5%.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' on page 5. Under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', for 'Recurrent mHSPC', the total for the Darolutamide arm is calculated by summing the 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' and 'Darolutamide (n = 154)' columns: 58 + 28 = 86. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is 86/651 = 13.2%.

Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' on page 5. Under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', for 'Recurrent mHSPC', the total for the Placebo arm is calculated by summing the 'Placebo (n = 508)' and 'Placebo (n = 146)' columns: 59 + 23 = 82. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is 82/654 = 12.5%.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, the document explicitly states: 'OS was the primary end point. Secondary efficacy end points included time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days.'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the study is "Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer...". The abstract states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that Darolutamide is the additional therapy given in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: The overall number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm with a Gleason score of 7 or less is not reported. Table 1 provides Gleason score distribution for Darolutamide (Treatment) and Placebo (Control) arms within specific subgroups (High Volume, Low Volume, High Risk, Low Risk), but not for the overall treatment arm. These subgroups are not mutually exclusive, so their values cannot be summed to derive the overall treatment arm total.

Type of Therapy.value: darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This describes the treatment provided to participants.

Treatment Class.value: Triplet therapy (Darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel)
Treatment Class.evidence: The 'CONTEXT' section on page 2 explicitly mentions 'Triplet therapy for predominantly de novo docetaxel fit metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer'. The 'METHODS' section on page 1 further specifies that patients were 'randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. Darolutamide is an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, as indicated in the 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1.

Race - N (%) | White | Treatment.evidence: The document provides race breakdown in Table 1 and Figure A1 for specific subgroups (e.g., High Volume, Low Volume, High Risk, Low Risk) within the treatment and control arms. The 'All Patients' column for race in Table 1 aggregates data for the 'High Volume' subgroup (Darolutamide n=497, Placebo n=508), not the total treatment (n=651) or control (n=654) arms. Therefore, the number and percentage of White participants for the overall treatment arm are not reported.
